claim: Context-only processing should remain Pareto-preferable when all declared dependencies fit in the current invocation, cross-query reuse is absent, and state lifecycle overhead is positive
target_workload: Short stable tasks with no cross-invocation obligation
pre_outcome_observables: declared dependency horizon; cross-query reuse count; mutation rate; context capacity; lifecycle prices
intervention_or_comparison: context-only versus at least two non-isomorphic stateful implementations under matched model and visible evidence
eligible_implementation_classes: context-only; external governed store; internal recurrent or compressive state
matching_regime: reuse <= 1; no required fact outside the invocation; no mutation or revocation obligation
primary_outcome: contract pass rate and full lifecycle resource vector
expected_ordering: context-only is quality-noninferior and strictly better on at least one resource coordinate
falsification_margin: quality difference within 1 percentage point and one preregistered resource improvement
counterexample: State construction is amortized by hidden reuse or a required dependency actually crosses invocations
decisive_falsifier: A stateful class improves contract pass rate by more than 1 percentage point with no worse resource coordinate in both non-isomorphic families
closest_prior_art: Agent Memory; Agent-Native Memory; Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents; Shared Selective Persistent Memory

claim: A current-version and provenance contract becomes infeasible for a context-only frozen model when decision-critical updates are absent from every available invocation and cannot be recovered from parameters
target_workload: Non-parametric changing facts with explicit currentness and provenance obligations
pre_outcome_observables: update visibility; supersession rate; validity window; provenance requirement; frozen-model declaration
intervention_or_comparison: withhold the current update from context-only while exposing the same update through two governed-state realizations
eligible_implementation_classes: context-only; external bitemporal store; append-only event log or governed recurrent state
matching_regime: at least one held-out update is outside the model cutoff and outside the current context; current-version tolerance >= 99 percent
primary_outcome: current-version contract compliance and provenance trace validity
expected_ordering: context-only violates the semantic contract; topology-neutral governed state may remain feasible
falsification_margin: one or more verified stale answers violates the zero-silent-staleness contract
counterexample: The supposedly absent update leaks through the query, benchmark construction, or model training data
decisive_falsifier: Context-only satisfies the held-out currentness and provenance contract without access to the update or leakage
closest_prior_art: ClawVM; TOKI; STALE; Supersede; State-Aware Runtime

claim: Bounded adaptive computation should beat fixed computation only when a pre-outcome signal identifies heterogeneous marginal value of reasoning strongly enough to repay routing overhead
target_workload: Mixed-difficulty tasks with controlled evidence sufficiency
pre_outcome_observables: difficulty-proxy calibration; signal coverage; routing overhead; compute prices; latency SLO
intervention_or_comparison: fixed low and fixed high compute versus a cost-conditioned router under identical evidence and model family
eligible_implementation_classes: early exit or dynamic depth; external reasoning policy; solver or model router
matching_regime: difficulty strata fixed before outcomes; proxy AUC target >= 0.70; router overhead charged
primary_outcome: quality at matched cost and cost at quality noninferiority
expected_ordering: adaptive compute reduces charged cost by at least 15 percent at no more than 1 percentage point quality loss
falsification_margin: failure to meet either the 15 percent cost margin or 1 point noninferiority margin
counterexample: Homogeneous difficulty or a shifted proxy makes fixed compute superior
decisive_falsifier: Across three model families the best fixed policy weakly dominates the adaptive policy on quality and every charged resource coordinate
closest_prior_art: Rational Metareasoning; X-Router; Duo-LLM; ATLAS test-time allocation

claim: A state-validity gate before compute allocation should dominate a difficulty-only router when persistent state is stale, missing, or contradictory
target_workload: Longitudinal tasks with independently controlled state validity and reasoning difficulty
pre_outcome_observables: state-validity label hidden from the model; stale or missing rate; difficulty stratum; ex ante router signals; compute price
intervention_or_comparison: factorial intervention on state validity by fixed or adaptive compute; compare validity-aware and difficulty-only routers
eligible_implementation_classes: external temporal store; append-only event store; internal recurrent or parametric state with an independent validity oracle
matching_regime: at least 25 percent invalid-state cases and balanced easy or hard strata; all validity labels generated independently of either router
primary_outcome: contract pass rate and charged compute with state-validity by compute interaction
expected_ordering: extra compute helps valid hard cases but not invalid-state cases; the validity-aware router improves cost at matched compliance
falsification_margin: interaction confidence interval excludes zero and validity-aware routing saves at least 15 percent compute at no more than 1 point compliance loss
counterexample: Reasoning reconstructs the correct current state from available evidence or the validity check costs more than it saves
decisive_falsifier: No replicated interaction in three model families or the difficulty-only router weakly dominates after all validation costs are charged
closest_prior_art: STALE; X-Router; PLACEMEM; PersistBench; value-of-computation metareasoning

claim: A transition rule must abstain when its pre-outcome features do not identify contract feasibility or Pareto ordering under the declared uncertainty set
pre_outcome_observables: coverage score; confidence interval width; calibration error; overlap of Pareto uncertainty sets
intervention_or_comparison: forced decision versus abstaining decision under held-out family and workload shift
eligible_implementation_classes: any two non-isomorphic state classes and fixed or adaptive compute classes
matching_regime: coverage below 90 percent or overlapping preregistered uncertainty sets
expected_ordering: abstention keeps conditional violation risk below the declared bound while forced decisions exceed it
falsification_margin: upper confidence bound on conditional violation risk must remain below the contract threshold
counterexample: A forced simple policy generalizes with lower total loss after abstention cost
decisive_falsifier: The non-abstaining comparator meets the same risk bound with strictly lower total cost on two held-out implementation families
closest_prior_art: robust decision and reject-option theory; ClawVM explicit infeasibility; workload-dependent systems studies
